claim: Expert-workflow rubrics showed higher cross-LLM agreement than generic rubrics in the reported Figma comparison.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Support a controlled pilot; do not attribute the difference specifically to skill grounding or expert authorship.

claim: Generic rubrics produced lower reported inter-judge agreement in the same Figma runs.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Provides the comparison baseline while disconfirming any isolated causal claim about expert authorship.

claim: Expert-workflow rubrics showed lower reported judge variance.
decision_use: Support testing reliability, but not adoption without criterion-validity evidence.

claim: Generic rubrics showed higher reported judge variance.
decision_use: Provides the variance baseline and reinforces that reliability alone is insufficient.

claim: Automated output grades had weak concordance with human judgments at the individual-run level.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Disconfirm readiness for immediate adoption and motivate a multi-expert validity gate.

claim: Automated skill grades had weak concordance with human judgments at the individual-run level.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Disconfirm readiness for immediate adoption and require independent process annotation.

claim: The skill-execution ablation showed heterogeneous directional effects in a very small bundled comparison.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Treat execution evidence as exploratory; do not use it as a causal justification for scoring adoption.

claim: One configured system had a large reported directional skill gain.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Supports investigating benefits in a controlled pilot, not broad adoption.

claim: Only a minority of no-skill runs deployed in the small paired comparison.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Suggests a hypothesis for a separately controlled execution study, while disconfirming a clean procedure-causality claim.

claim: Pairwise preference did not consistently clear the reported expert readiness threshold.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Require an absolute, multi-expert readiness gate rather than adopting on relative wins.
